pattern: Count occurrences of every value, then for each value combine its count with the count of the value one greater to size the best harmonious group.
hints: A harmonious subsequence uses only two distinct values that differ by exactly one.
Tally how many times each number appears; order does not matter for a subsequence.
For every value v that has at least one neighbor v+1 present, the candidate length is count[v] + count[v+1]; take the maximum, or zero if no such pair exists.

edge_inputs.description: single element cannot form a pair of differing values
all identical so no value plus one exists
mixed counts where the best pair dominates
consecutive distinct values each pair length two
gap of two never forms a harmonious pair
must add neighbor counts, not just the larger single count
two separate harmonious bands, larger one wins

stress.description: many values clustered in a narrow band to force overlapping pairs
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    return [[rng.randint(0, 15) for _ in range(size)]]
